perturbation.original_text: FASC agrees to provide to the Adviser the services indicated in Exhibit A to this Agreement (the "Services").
perturbation.changed_text: FASC agrees to provide to the Adviser the services indicated in Exhibit A to this Agreement (the "Services"), contingent upon resource availability and prioritization as determined by FASC.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by making the provision of services contingent upon FASC's internal resource availability and prioritization, which contradicts the initial agreement that FASC 'agrees to provide' the services. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 1 where the language is no longer a firm commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Services. FASC agrees to provide to the Adviser the services indicated in Exhibit A to this Agreement (the "Services"), contingent upon resource availability and prioritization as determined by FASC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contingency ('contingent upon resource availability and prioritization') to the service provision obligation, while Section 1, now containing this changed text, initially presents the service provision as an agreement without such contingencies. This creates an in-text contradiction because FASC's obligation is no longer a firm commitment, making the term ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party hereto, except in cases of unforeseen circumstances where termination can occur immediately.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'except in cases of unforeseen circumstances where termination can occur immediately' directly contradicts the requirement of a 60-day written notice for termination. It creates ambiguity as to what constitutes 'unforeseen circumstances' and how such circumstances would override the standard termination clause, potentially leading to legal disputes. This affects the rest of Section 5 because any other part that refers to the termination being subject to 60 days would be contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Duration and Termination. (a) Subject to the remaining provisions of this Section, the term of this Agreement shall begin on the effective date first above written and shall continue until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto or by either party on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party hereto, except in cases of unforeseen circumstances where termination can occur immediately.
perturbation.justification: The added clause regarding 'unforeseen circumstances where termination can occur immediately' directly contradicts the originally stated requirement of a 60-day written notice for termination. This creates a significant ambiguity regarding the termination process, as it introduces an exception without clearly defining 'unforeseen circumstances' or outlining the procedure for immediate termination. This violates the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction because the contract has key terms that are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.
